reasoning: The most recent FRED data shows the 20-year TIPS yield ending at 2.33 on Friday, December 5, 2025. This sets a relatively high baseline. Expectations for the December 9-10 FOMC meeting have swung toward an 87% probability of a 25 basis point rate cut. A rate cut may provide near-term downward pressure (or at least stabilization) on real yields, contributing to a likely mean reversion from the recent spike (from 2.22 to 2.33 over the past two weeks). Consequently, the probability of yields being strictly higher than 2.33 by the December 14 resolution date is assessed below 50%. As we move further out to January and March 2026, the variance increases significantly (approaching a random walk) and uncertainties regarding macroeconomic trends, fiscal policy, and inflation dominate, naturally pulling the probabilities closer to 0.50.
